desc:
This narrow ledge has a dizzying view of the cirque to the north. The valley
contains a dark lake that almost totally fills the bottom of the bowl-shaped
area. To the west the ledge quickly peters out, but to the south it is possible
to maneuver among the rocky formations.
name: A Rocky Ledge

desc:
To the north, this narrow trail descends to a relatively wide ledge over-
looking a bowl-shaped valley. The valley below contains a dark lake - only a
narrow stretch of land along the northern edge allows access to the head of the
valley. To the south, the trail climbs upward to the crest of a ridge, while to
the west a narrow ledge clings to the cliff wall.
name: The Goats Trail

desc:
It is not unusual to find goats this high up in the mountains and you can follow
the muddy goats trail around the back of the pinnacle. The trail is still
climbing high onto the back of Celebdil. There is snow on the ground.
name: The Goats Trail

desc:
To the north-east there is a large stack of rock extending to the sky for
several hundreds of feet. There is no way over it, you will have to go around
one way or the other.
name: Mountainous Terrain

desc:
This high on the slopes of Celebdil, the world is encased in a permanent blanket
of snow and ice. The air grows thinner and more difficult to breathe. Each step
upward is more and more of an exertion, and the narrow trail still climbs above.
name: The Snow Line

desc:
The footing on this trail is treacherous, as the rocks are covered in a thin
casing of ice. Only a few hardy lichens subsist on the occasional patches of
exposed rocky soil, and even the mountain goats seem to shun this harsh area.
The trail continues leading steadily upward, toward an unknown destination,
while below it passes into a milder clime.
name: Icy Trail

desc:
High on the shoulder of a spur of Celebdil, this ice-covered ledge is buffeted
by high winds. A small cavern to the east offers a modicum of shelter from the
harsh climate. A narrow trail leads down the side of the mountain, but it
appears to be impossible to scale the cliffs above. Far below to the west, the
hills of Eregion can be seen in the distance.
name: Icy Ledge

desc:
This cave, high on the flank of Celebdil, is apparently the home of some
intelligent creature, for there is a small fire burning here in a small circle
of stones. There is no hint as to what would motivate someone to live in such a
harsh locale, and whoever does dwell here must have a difficult life.
name: Rimy Cave

desc:
The room is essentially a natural cavern - it has had little if any work done to
improve upon its natural state. The walls are cold stone that is encrusted in
many places by a thin layer of ice. The temperature here is just below freezing,
and this place is apparently used as cold storage for perishable food by whoever
inhabits this place.
name: Storage Room

desc:
The wide area atop the cliffs over Durin's Gate narrows quickly to the south.
Directly below is the cirque that forms the head of the valley of the Sirannon -
except for a narrow strip of land along the northeast portion, it is flooded
with dark water. The ground is littered with droppings of goats and elk, and
their frequent wanderings have worn a trail leading up to the southwest.
name: The Top of Moria Walls

desc:
Dark and silent, the smooth grey cliffs rise from the water of the lake, looming
overhead with a forbidding solemnity. The cliffside to the east is smooth and
unbroken by any seam or crack. A narrow strip of dry land clings to the base of
the cliff - cluttered with fallen rock and rubble, it provides a means of
skirting the dark lake to the west.
name: Under a Grey Stone Cliff

desc:
A damp and murky tunnel has been dug under the mountain. The walls and floor are
covered with mould and slime due to the dampness in the air. The ceiling is very
low and there is not much room for movement. The air is stuffy and unclean. The
tunnel continues to the south.
name: A Tunnel in the Mountain

desc:
A damp and murky tunnel has been dug under the mountain. The walls and floor are
covered with mould and slime due to the dampness in the air. The ceiling is very
low and there is not much room for movement. The air is stuffy and unclean. The
tunnel continues to the south.
name: A Tunnel in the Mountain

desc:
Here the tunnel makes an abrupt bend. Gnawing sounds come from the darkness and
echo from the walls. No daylight penetrates into this tunnel system, and all
connections with the upper world seem severed. The tunnel continues to the west
and to the north.
name: A Murky Tunnel

desc:
The wide tunnel ends here, and all that remains of it is a small and narrow
crawlway that leads north into the darkness. The floor under your feet is
slippery, and the walls drip with moisture. A stench of decaying flesh and
rotting cadavers attacks your nose.
name: A Murky Tunnel

desc:
This is a gargantuan cavern, the surrounding walls are so far apart that from
one side the other side seems non-existent. Skulls and bones from all kinds of
creatures are strewn on the floor, traces of dried and matted blood are also
evident on the ground. Rotted wooden and rusted metal equipment are heaped in
small piles all over the place. The entire cavern carries the stink of death.
name: A Great Cavern

desc:
This tiny cave is little more than a small rocky niche, and its walls and
ceiling are cracked all over. Tiny rivulets of ice-cold water trickle through
many of the deeper fissures, gathering in a small pool on the floor, which
overflows at one end, to run out of the cavern through a larger crack.
name: In a Cave behind the Waterfall

desc:
From this vantage it is clear that this is no mere mountain. There is no doubt
that these caverns and corridors were formed by a great eruption of molten lava.
The hot liquid burned through the mountainous rock on its journey to escape
through the crack in the mountain's summit. In some places, such as this one,
the lava's path was halted and the pressure formed a cavernous hole.
name: Volcanic Cavern

desc:
This place resembles a great staircase as it is home to a cascade of uneven
steps. Some are only a few feet wide, others much larger. What began as a fast
flowing stream of lava, here slowed and formed these steps with each hesitation.
The surface of each step is rough and charred, with small holes where the hot
liquid seeped farther into the ground.
name: Cinder Steps

desc:
Even holding a torch, a non-nocturnal creature, venturing into the dark cave,
could not see more than a few feet in front of themselves. The walls, burned
black by the violent fires of this volcano, reflect firelight no more. They have
the texture of rock scalded smooth. The ground is covered with a layer of ash.
name: Charcoal Alcove

desc:
This place is even more lacking in light than the cavern above. The walls, floor
and ceiling are a deep black ash colour. It is difficult to discern for certain
but one can estimate being about half a mile down from the crack in the summit.
Just as in the chamber above, no plant life can be found.
name: Descending into the Depths

desc:
Pools of lava unable to burst free formed these large black chambers. Where the
floor meets the walls, the ground curls upward unlike structures made by mortals
that have sharp corners. The cavern resembles the inside of a bubble of blown
glass, fittingly so since both are created by intense heat.
name: Scorched Chamber

desc:
The walls of this cavern are as smooth as glass. Molten lava from the volcanic
eruption pooled here and waited for a fissure to open allowing it to continue
its upward journey. The intense heat cauterized and blackened the walls of this
chamber. There seem to be some cracks in the floor toward the west.
name: Scorched Chamber

desc:
Pulverised bone covers the floor of this chamber like chalk dust. Only a few
stray bones stacked up against an inner wall are left intact. Amongst the dust
lie the remnants of what must have been the armour of the owners of these bones.
Small shredded pieces of metal and wood beg the question if anything belonging
to those vanquished here could be left in its entirety hidden among the remains.
On one wall, there is a strange indentation that merits closer investigation.
Another wall seems to bulge outward in a similar shape.
name: Chamber of the Beast

desc:
The gashes in the passage walls look to have been made by claws and the sharp
slashing of evil weapons. The depth of the cuts gives evidence that whatever
made such marks did so quickly with strong and eager sense of purpose. It is
also evident, by the haphazard nature of the construction, they were carved with
little regard for giving the tunnels any structural integrity.
name: Northern Passage

desc:
The crumbling bits of this passage's ceiling show those building it gave no
consideration to the weight of the mountain above. It appears that it was not
long after dirt and rock that once filled this place was removed, that the
strain on the ceiling became too much. The blockage of the passage northward
appears to have occurred about the same time as construction judging by the
amount of ash covering the rock. Air escapes through the cracks. Peering into
those same cracks one can see the passage once continued farther northward.
name: End of the Passage

desc:
The cave opens up from the narrow entrance. A tunnel-like descent leads down to
the depths of the mountain. A small trickle of water drops on the floor from the
condensation on the ceiling. The cave appears as if it had been carved on
purpose, but neither by nature nor time. The cave walls seem slimy and are cold
if touched.
name: Dirty Cave

desc:
The filthy cavern is wide, its walls jagged, covered with slimy algae of some
sort. The floor is covered with refuse and decaying remains of corpses. Besides
the thick and nauseating stench, the air is stale and thin. A few small puddles
of water are on the ground, filling small holes in the otherwise plain cave
floor.
name: A Dark Cavern

desc:
The dark subterranean passage contains a liquid as cold and viscous as if it has
poured through the mountain since the day it was carved out from the filthy
earth. The distinctive currents breaking through the cracked foundation agitate
an unnumbered amount of particles that struggle to fit between the sharp and
sedimented walls of the cavity. Embedded in a bleary breadth of view, the sludge
is accompanied by the constant sound of splattering, making it extremely
difficult to perceive the surroundings.
name: Turbulent Sludge

desc:
The air is a bit stale in the vast cavernous hole. The walls are as black as
night. This inner sanctum appears to stretch for miles across and the entrance
crack above is too high to let in any illumination. The ground is covered in a
black charred material that crumbles easily. Under that layer is a black
substance hard as rock. Nothing grows here, not even moss or mildew.
name: Descending into the Depths
